jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams] followed by glass shattering and panting
jokes.setup: Homer's signature exclamation suggests another mishap
jokes.punchline: The escalating sound effects of destruction
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase perfectly integrated with implied physical comedy destruction

jokes.text: Human roaches, feeding off each other's garbage. The only thing you can't buy here is dignity.
jokes.setup: Introducing the Springfield Swap Meet with harsh imagery
jokes.punchline: The dignity line as a perfect summation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational comedy with perfect economy - captures swap meet atmosphere with dark wit

jokes.text: Ich bin ein Springfield Swap Meet patron.
jokes.punchline: Applied to mundane swap meet attendance
jokes.explanation: Solid cultural reference joke with appropriate character delivery from CBG

jokes.text: Hand painted to resemble Lucille Ball! You'll love Oyster Lucy! [Deep Voice] Oh, Mr. Mooney, I just gotta meet Bob Cummings! Viv!
jokes.punchline: Doing I Love Lucy impressions to sell them
jokes.explanation: Absurd swap meet merchandise concept with committed voice work

jokes.text: Joseph of Aramathea! Twenty-six conversions in A.D. 46!
jokes.setup: Religious trading cards as parody of sports cards
jokes.punchline: Treating religious figures like baseball statistics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept treating religion like sports statistics - highly original

jokes.text: Whoa! A Methuselah rookie card! Who'd have thought learning about religion could be fun?
jokes.setup: Continuing the religious trading card concept
jokes.punchline: Methuselah as a 'rookie' despite his age, plus ironic education comment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Bart with layered joke - rookie card concept plus ironic learning comment

jokes.text: Religion? Learning? Let's get outta here!
jokes.punchline: Immediate retreat when they realize what they're doing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency - kids fleeing education despite momentary interest

jokes.text: Yeah, they took her off the market after some kid put both his eyes out.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of horrific injury as selling point
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about unsafe vintage toys delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Prisoner 24601. I wore this for two years in a Vietcong internment center. Never thought I'd see the old girl again. [Voice Resonating] Still fits.
jokes.setup: Skinner trying on old military gear
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic reunion with prison uniform, voice change from tight fit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment combining his military past with physical comedy of tight uniform

jokes.text: Small world, huh? It is. It really, really is.
jokes.punchline: Overly sincere, literal agreement
jokes.explanation: Subtle character comedy showing cops' literal-mindedness

jokes.text: The airplane's upside-down. Stradi-'who'-vius?
jokes.setup: Lisa correcting Homer about violin maker
jokes.punchline: Homer's mispronunciation emphasizing ignorance
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer cultural ignorance moment with efficient delivery

jokes.text: Your teenage son or daughter will think this wishbone necklace is really cool. I doubt my son or daughter is that stupid.
jokes.setup: Vendor's sales pitch about cool teenagers
jokes.punchline: Blunt dismissal of vendor's assessment
jokes.explanation: Standard setup-punchline about tacky merchandise with decent timing

jokes.text: Melvin and the Squirrels. Part of the rodent invasion of the early '60s.
jokes.punchline: Treating novelty animal acts as literal invasion
jokes.explanation: Clever historical joke treating novelty acts as invasion force

jokes.text: Dad, thanks to television, I can't remember what happened eight minutes ago. No, really. I can't. It's a serious problem.
jokes.setup: Bart can't remember Homer's recording career
jokes.punchline: Blaming TV for memory loss, then forgetting he was joking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about TV's effect on attention spans with perfect escalation

jokes.text: What are we all laughing about? Who cares?
jokes.setup: Everyone laughing at Bart's memory joke
jokes.punchline: Already forgotten what they're laughing about
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback demonstrating the memory problem in real-time with economy

jokes.text: A maturing Joe Piscopo left Saturday Night Live to conquer Hollywood.
jokes.punchline: Calling Piscopo 'maturing' and suggesting he conquered Hollywood
jokes.explanation: Subtle dig at Piscopo's post-SNL career trajectory

jokes.text: Barbershop? That ain't been popular since aught-six, dag-nab it!
jokes.punchline: Old-timey speech pattern while dismissing old-timey music
jokes.explanation: Character comedy - old man using period language to criticize period music

jokes.text: What did I tell you? No talking like a grizzled 1890s prospector. Consarn it.
jokes.punchline: Using old-timey expression while forbidding it
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - character contradicts herself while delivering the correction

jokes.text: Anyway, rock and roll had become stagnant. 'Achy Breaky Heart' was seven years away. Something had to fill the void.
jokes.punchline: Treating 'Achy Breaky Heart' as musical salvation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic treating a widely mocked song as inevitable musical evolution

jokes.text: That's my son up there! What, the balding fat-ass? Uh, no, the Hindu guy.
jokes.setup: Father proudly pointing out son in quartet
jokes.punchline: Mistaken identity correction emphasizing ethnic difference
jokes.explanation: Standard mistaken identity joke with decent character specificity

jokes.text: I'm gonna nail that cop right between the eyes. Right after this song is over.
jokes.punchline: Politely waiting for song to finish
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between criminal intent and social courtesy

jokes.text: Ned, there's an oil stain in the parking lot that looks just like St. Barnabus.
jokes.setup: Ned pointing out inappropriate song choice
jokes.punchline: Reverend distracting with absurd religious 'miracle'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Reverend avoiding confrontation with religious nonsense

jokes.text: All except that police officer. Yuck! Too Village People. You'll have to replace him.
jokes.setup: Agent wanting to represent the quartet
jokes.punchline: Rejecting Wiggum for being too stereotypically gay-coded
jokes.explanation: Sharp entertainment industry observation about image management

jokes.text: Where we goin'? Where we goin'? Run along, boy. You're free now. No! No! Noooo!
jokes.setup: Wiggum being driven away to be replaced
jokes.punchline: Dramatic abandonment like a pet being released
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy treating human like abandoned animal with Wiggum's characteristic patheticness

jokes.text: Eh, never fit on a marquis, luv. From now on your name is Apu de Beaumarche.
jokes.setup: Agent dealing with Apu's long Indian name
jokes.punchline: Replacing it with pretentious French name
jokes.explanation: Good satire of entertainment industry name changes and cultural erasure

jokes.text: It is a great dishonor to my ancestors and my god, but okay.
jokes.setup: Being forced to change his name
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging the offense but going along anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu moment - cultural dignity versus practical accommodation with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Get off the stage! I want to, but I can't!
jokes.punchline: Literally unable to stop performing
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy gag about performer being literally stuck

jokes.text: Good Lord! Doctor Doolittle is Chief Wiggum.
jokes.setup: Terrible auditioner singing Dr. Doolittle songs
jokes.punchline: Recognizing it's actually Wiggum in disguise
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Wiggum's dismissal with character-appropriate disguise attempt

jokes.text: Barney! How'd you like to sing for our group? Sure! Why not? Now whar's me toothpick?
jokes.punchline: Immediately reverting to slovenly behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney character moment - hidden talent immediately undercut by crude behavior

jokes.text: How about Handsome Homer Simpson, Plus Three? I like it!
jokes.punchline: Homer's narcissistic suggestion getting support
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer egotism with Skinner's characteristic agreeableness

jokes.text: No. We need a name that's witty at first... but seems less funny each time you hear it. How about the Be Sharps?
jokes.punchline: Perfectly describing the joke itself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke that perfectly describes its own comedic trajectory - exceptional writing

jokes.text: I bought this cool pencil holder. Far out, man. I haven't seen a bong in years.
jokes.punchline: Homer recognizing drug paraphernalia
jokes.explanation: Excellent character contrast - Lisa's innocence versus Homer's experience with perfect economy

jokes.text: One of us made some money. I sold a guy our spare tire. [Tire Blows] D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Immediate consequence of poor decision
jokes.explanation: Classic setup-punchline with perfect timing and Homer's signature catchphrase

jokes.text: Mmm. Baby on board. 'Baby on board' 'Something, something Burt Ward' This thing writes itself!
jokes.setup: Homer seeing baby on board sign
jokes.punchline: Lazy songwriting process with random rhyme
jokes.explanation: Great satire of lazy songwriting with Homer's characteristic approach to creativity

jokes.text: Oh, yes. Bouffant Betty. Well, I would prefer we kept your marriage a secret. A lot of women are going to want to have sex with you... and, uh, we want them to think they can.
jokes.setup: Homer mentioning telling Marge about success
jokes.punchline: Agent's blunt explanation of marketing strategy
jokes.explanation: Sharp entertainment industry satire about manufactured celebrity appeal

jokes.text: Well, if I explain it to Marge that way, I'm sure she'll understand. [Marge sobbing]
jokes.punchline: Cut to Marge clearly not understanding/devastated
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony with economical visual punchline

jokes.text: That little boy who nobody liked grew up to be... Roy Cohn. Wow! And now you know the rest of the story.
jokes.punchline: Revealing universally despised historical figure
jokes.explanation: Excellent parody subverting Paul Harvey's typically uplifting reveals with dark historical figure

jokes.text: Aw, squiddy, I got nothing against ya. I just heard there was gold in your belly. Ha, ha, harr! Ha, ha, harr!
jokes.punchline: Apologetic explanation for violence with pirate laugh
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sea Captain moment combining politeness with piratical greed

jokes.text: Have you seen Bart? I stuck him somewhere. [Clanking] Look what I got for you, Dad.
jokes.punchline: Bart trapped somewhere making noise
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's casual neglect of parental duties

jokes.text: Isn't it true that you're really an Indian? By the many arms of Vishnu, I swear it is a lie. Barney, how did you join the group? They found me on the men's room floor.
jokes.punchline: Apu's obviously false denial and Barney's honest degradation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moments - Apu's obvious lie contrasted with Barney's brutal honesty

jokes.text: You've been referred to as 'the funny one.' Is that reputation justified? Yes. Yes, it is.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking about Skinner's comedic reputation
jokes.punchline: Completely unfunny, serious confirmation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anti-joke perfectly capturing Skinner's humorless personality

jokes.text: She's 100 years old, and she weighs over 200 tons. This enormous woman will devour us all! Aaah! I-- I meant the statue.
jokes.setup: Homer dedicating song to special woman
jokes.punchline: Crowd panicking, then awkward clarification
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and payoff with Homer's characteristic poor communication

jokes.text: Okay. Gilligan, the Skipper, and Chief Wiggum. Name three castaways. Hi-oh!
jokes.punchline: Including Wiggum as if he's from Gilligan's Island
jokes.explanation: Decent callback joke treating Wiggum as fictional character

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Oh, my God! Where did you get that brownie? There's a big pile of'em. [Squealing]
jokes.punchline: More interested in pot brownies than the Beatle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - missing cultural significance for immediate gratification

jokes.text: You're a musician? What a nice fellow.
jokes.setup: George Harrison asking if Homer likes his music
jokes.punchline: Homer completely unaware of who he's talking to
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer obliviousness moment with perfect character consistency

jokes.text: You beat Dexy's Midnight Runners. You haven't heard the last of them.
jokes.punchline: Ominous threat from one-hit wonder band
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist threat from famously brief music career

jokes.text: They took the foam off the market because they found out it was poisonous. But if you ask me, if you're dumb enough to eat it, you deserve to die.
jokes.punchline: Casual endorsement of natural selection
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about product safety with blunt social Darwinism

jokes.text: What'd you do? Screw up like the Beatles and say you were bigger than Jesus? All the time. It was the title of our second album.
jokes.punchline: Homer casually admitting to worse behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Beatles reference with Homer's characteristic obliviousness to consequences

jokes.text: What about Bob Hope? He's been consistently popular for over 50 years. So has Sinatra. Well, anyway, we were all getting tired of-- Dean Martin still packs 'em in. Ditto Tom Jones. Shut up!
jokes.punchline: Lisa systematically destroying his theory
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating structure with perfect Lisa-Homer dynamic - her logic versus his nonsense

jokes.text: This is worse than your song about Mr. T. I pity the fool who doesn't like... he.
jokes.punchline: Awkward forced rhyme with 'he'
jokes.explanation: Good example of Homer's terrible songwriting with awkward grammar for rhyme

jokes.text: Barbershop is in danger of growing stale. I'm taking it to strange new places. Number eight. [Belch]
jokes.setup: Barney's artistic pretensions with conceptual artist
jokes.punchline: Belching as avant-garde performance
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of pretentious art world with perfect Barney character integration

jokes.text: How much is this quart of milk? Twelve dollars. Hey, Barney, what'll it be? I'd like a beer, Moe. I'd like a single plum floating in perfume, served in a man's hat.
jokes.punchline: Barney's pretentious drink order from art world experience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist callback showing lasting impact of art world on Barney - perfectly quotable

jokes.text: I'd like a beer, Moe. I'd like a single plum floating in perfume, served in a man's hat.
jokes.punchline: Completely bizarre artistic drink request
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd artistic pretension - incredibly specific and bizarre

jokes.text: Hey, fellas. I'm back. Oh, that's great. Your replacement is getting tired. [Clucks] Hey, Queenie, you can go now. I'll give her a good home. And I did.
jokes.setup: Barney returning to regular bar routine
jokes.punchline: Chicken was his replacement, Homer adopts it
jokes.explanation: Absurdist resolution with Homer's characteristic animal adoption tendency

jokes.text: Last year, on that stupid Dame Edna special.
jokes.setup: When they were last all together
jokes.punchline: Embarrassing low-point variety show appearance
jokes.explanation: Good reference to has-been celebrity trajectory via embarrassing variety show

jokes.text: Hello! Human Fly here. Come on! I stayed up all night dyeing my underwear.
jokes.setup: Superhero wannabe trying to get attention
jokes.punchline: Pathetic dedication to terrible costume
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy about pathetic attention-seeking with specific detail

jokes.text: Wait a minute. There's nothing in here about the Be Sharps! Come back here! Ha-ha!
jokes.punchline: Scammed by fake newsboy
jokes.explanation: Standard con-man gag with Homer as predictable victim

jokes.text: It's been done. [Tires Screech]
jokes.punchline: Dismissive reference to famous precedent
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-commentary on Beatles homage with maximum economy

jokes.text: I'd like to thank you on behalf of the group... and I hope we passed the audition. I don't get it.
jokes.setup: Homer quoting John Lennon's famous rooftop line
jokes.punchline: Barney missing the Beatles reference entirely
jokes.explanation: Good Beatles reference joke with perfect Barney obliviousness as punchline
